Title: The first intron and promoter of Arabidopsis DIACYLGLYCEROL ACYLTRANSFERASE 1 exert synergistic effects on pollen and embryo lipid accumulation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Carbon and energy storage; reproductive development support
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured by RT-qPCR in flowers in this study; detailed pattern not established as core focus
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PDAT1 provides overlapping TAG biosynthetic function with DGAT1; combined loss with dgat1-1 is gametophyte lethal, and restoration of DGAT1 expression rescues the segregation defect at the PDAT1/pdat1-2 locus
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PDAT1 function overlaps with DGAT1 in Arabidopsis TAG synthesis. 2. The dgat1-1/pdat1-2 combination causes pollen/gametophytic lethality, producing non-Mendelian segregation. 3. Rescue of this phenotype by properly regulated AtDGAT1 confirms PDAT1 serves as the parallel TAG-producing activity whose absence reveals DGAT1 regulatory insufficiency.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic segregation analysis of PDAT1/pdat1-2 in complemented lines; allele-specific PCR genotyping; phenotypic rescue tests in dgat1-1 background
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss together with DGAT1 causes gametophyte lethality; pdat1-2 single mutant reportedly shows no major seed-oil phenotype
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant partner gene for stacking/replacement strategies in TAG engineering; should be considered when replacing endogenous TAG-synthesis capacity to avoid fertility defects
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to the genetic system, PDAT1 is not the primary subject of mechanistic dissection in the Results; it functions mainly as the overlapping TAG-biosynthetic comparator/unmasking background.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Carbon and energy storage; required for pollen viability/development and embryo oil accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Acyl-CoA-dependent terminal acylation step of TAG assembly (Kennedy-pathway-type DGAT step)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl group from acyl-CoA to diacylglycerol to form triacylglycerol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control/gatekeeper enzyme for TAG accumulation, as described in the paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in flowers/pollen and developing embryos/seeds; paper emphasizes requirement during pollen and embryo development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in developing whole flowers; proper temporal/tissue-specific expression requires native promoter plus first intron
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Native AtDGAT1 driven by its promoter plus first intron restores near-wild-type pollen development, silique fertility, seed yield, and seed oil accumulation; omission of the first intron causes severe pollen defects, reduced seed set, and reduced total seed oil despite near-wild-type fatty acid composition
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtDGAT1 is essential for normal TAG accumulation supporting pollen viability and embryo oil deposition. 2. A transgene containing the 2066 bp promoter plus the first intron largely complements the dgat1-1 pdat1-2 synthetic-lethal reproductive phenotype and restores Mendelian segregation of PDAT1/pdat1-2. 3. A construct lacking the first intron only partially complements: anthers often lack pollen, seed yield is strongly reduced, siliques abort, and seed oil remains reduced similarly to dgat1-1. 4. Both intron-containing and intron-lacking constructs restore seed fatty acid composition toward wild type, indicating catalytic DGAT1 function is present, but correct expression level/timing depends on the first intron plus promoter context.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic complementation in dgat1-1/dgat1-1//PDAT1/pdat1-2 background; segregation/genotyping of PDAT1 locus; seed yield and silique fertility analysis; pollen staining and pollen tube assays; seed lipid/FAME profiling; RT-qPCR; in silico cis-element analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard growth chamber conditions (20–23°C, continuous light); no special environmental treatment central to metabolic interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native AtDGAT1 promoter (2066 bp; endogenous/tissue-context-dependent); At2S-3 promoter used in prior construct context only
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Without the first intron, transgenic complementation causes strong fertility defects, pollen loss, silique abortion, low seed yield, and reduced seed oil; even +intron lines do not fully restore total oil to wild type
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: In silico TF-binding analysis using public DAP-seq context; no new omics accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: The AtDGAT1 promoter plus first intron defines a minimal regulatory module for expressing endogenous or heterologous DGATs with proper reproductive and seed-oil expression, useful for designer oil engineering while minimizing fertility penalties
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper focuses as much on cis-regulation of DGAT1 as on enzyme activity; the decisive finding is that promoter and first intron act synergistically to confer correct pollen/embryo expression. The tested constructs included E826 (-intron) and E827 (+intron), with earlier E546/E700/E702 constructs used to map regulatory sufficiency.
